Completely agree AI can be so helpful! I had a laparscopy in December and they found adhesions on my bowel and my ovary was adhered to my pelvic wall. No active endo but did find old pelvic scarring. They managed to release some adhesions on my bowel but couldn't do anything with my adheared ovary as it was risking my utera (I never know how to spell that 😂) I went back for my follow up in March and told my consultant I was still in lots of pain. She completely dismissed my pain and told me that my adheared ovary wouldn't cause me that much pain (I'm struggling to walk as I have sciatic type pain in my leg and my ovary literally feels like it's pulsating and being ripped out of my body!) She just told me to loose weight and the pain should pass! This didn't sit right with me so I used Chat GBT to deep dive into my issues. Through this, I was able to educate myself on a lot of things. It told me that I should be referred to an endo specialist as my laparscopy was incomplete, there was a risk to my utera (that word again!), even though she had released some adhesions on my bowel.. that should have been done by a specialist and that due to my ovary being adhered to my pelvic wall, it's classed as pelvic distortion. All of these fit the criteria to be referred to an endo specialist, which she should have done right after my surgery 🙄 I then asked Chat GBT to create a letter I can send to my GP requesting for a second opinion and to be seen by an endo specialist. The letter was brilliant and really clear. I sent it off not really expecting much back but within a week my GP called me and couldn't believe I had been dismissed like that and agreed with me that I should be referred to an endo specialist! She sent the referral off to the Endo specialist centre on Oxford as that's the closest place to. me and my referral has been accepted!! All this because of AI! I now have the long wait to be seen but I would rather wait it out to be seen by a specialist than a general gynocologist that thinks loosing weight is the only key to becoming pain free! (I'm 14 stone so not too overweight either!) So thank you AI!
You are your own biggest advocate 🫶🏻
